original: Hollie Roffey, aged 10 days, is the youngest person to ever receive a heart transplant and survives 28 days
NASA launches its Perseverance rover and Ingenuity helicopter to Mars aboard an Atlas V 541 rocket from Cape Canaveral, Florida
Vanuatu (New Hebrides) gains independence from Britain and France
First FIFA World Cup Final, Estadio Centenario, Montevideo, Uruguay: Uruguay beats Argentina 4-2 in the inaugural event
American swimmers Mary T. Meagher (2.06.37) and Craig Beardsley (1.58.21) set 200 m butterfly world records at the US National Swimming Championships at Irvine, California
Lin Li swims a female world record in the 200m medley with a time of 2:11.65

8.8 magnitude earthquake strikes off Russia's far eastern Kamchatka Peninsula, triggering tsunami warnings across the Pacific Ocean; evacuations ordered in parts of Japan and Hawaii, as well as alerts being issued in China, New Zealand, the Philippines, and other island countries
Tom Pidcock, English cyclist (Olympic gold mountain bike cross country 2020, 24; Cyclo-cross World C'ship gold 2022), born in Leeds, England
First rocket attains 100 mi (167 km) altitude in White Sands, New Mexico
FIFA World Cup Final, Wembley Stadium, London: Striker Geoff Hurst scores a hat trick as England beats West Germany 4-2 after extra time
Japan defeats Russia for Olympic gold in woman's volleyball
Expos crush Braves 19-0, collecting 28 hits and tying the National League record with eight home runs
In Bandar Mahshahr, Iran, the temperature reaches 46°C (115°F), but humidity makes it feel like 73°C (163°F)
Françoise Barré-Sinoussi, French virologist (Nobel Prize 2008, discovered HIV), born in Paris, France
Largest gathering of people with the same first name: 2,325 people named Ivan in Kupres, Bosnia and Herzegovina
Klyuchevskoy Volcano in Russia's Kamchatka Peninsula erupts
CaptainJames Cookwith HMS Resolution safely returns to England at the end of his second voyage
In his MLB debut, SF Giants future Baseball Hall of Fame first basemanWillie McCoveygoes 4-for-4 in a 7-2 win over Philadelphia Phillies at Seal Stadium

Toronto Rocks - SARSStock benefit with performers includingThe Rolling Stones,AC/DC, Rush, The Guess Who,Justin Timberlake, The Flaming Lips, and The Isley Brothers, at Downsview Park; largest concert in Canadian history attracts 450,000 spectators
Kate Bush, English singer-songwriter ("Running Up That Hill"; "Wuthering Heights"), born in Bexleyheath, Kent, England
US Apollo 15 (Scott & Irwin) lands on Mare Imbrium on the Moon
High-resolution images reveal intricate tattoos on a 2,500-year-old female Siberian 'ice mummy'
C. T. Vivian, American civil rights activist, worked with Martin Luther King, born in Boonville, Missouri (d. 2020)
Bartolomeo Rastrelli presents the newly built Catherine Palace to Empress Elizabeth and her courtiers in Russia
38th NFL Chicago All-Star Game: Baltimore 24, All-Stars 17 (52,289 attendees)
Discovery moves to the Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center in Florida for the mating of the STS 51-I mission
Record 103.8°F (39.9°C) in Preschen, Lusatia, Germany
Temperature of 41.2°C (106.2°F) in Tamba, Hyogo prefecture, Japan, sets new record for country, eclipsing previous high of 41.1°C set in 2018 and 2020; surpassed less than a week later

Former presidentBarack Obamagives the eulogy at the funeral of Georgia congressman and civil rights leaderJohn Lewis; former presidentsGeorge W. BushandBill Clintonalso speak at Ebenezer Baptist Church in Atlanta
Henry Louis Smith, American physicist who made the first X-ray photograph, born in Greensboro, North Carolina (d. 1951)
Clive Sinclair, British consumer electronics inventor (Executive pocket calculator; ZX Spectrum computer), born in Richmond, Surrey, England (d. 2021)[1]
Spanish gold and silver fleet disappears off St. Lucie, Florida
Dominic Cork takes a hat-trick in England's Test cricket win against West Indies at Old Trafford
Five hundred Marseillais men sing France's national anthem for the first time
Mahlon Loomis patents wireless telegraphy
Boston and Philadelphia are the first baseball teams to play outside the US in the British Isles
Évian Championship Women's Golf, Évian Resort GC: Céline Boutier of France wins her first major title by 6 strokes over Canada's defending champion Brooke Henderson
First Australian-made rocket by Gilmour Space Technologies launches in Queensland, Australia, for 14 seconds before crashing
Vladimir Zworykin, Russian-American inventor of the cathode-ray tube for television, born in Murom, Russia (d. 1982)
Joe Nuxhall, American baseball pitcher and sportscaster (youngest MLB player at 15 years, 316 days), born in Hamilton, Ohio (d. 2007)
Sid Krofft, Canadian puppeteer and television producer - with his brother Marty (H.R. Pufnstuf; Land of the Lost; Sigmund and the Sea Monsters; Donny & Marie), born in Montreal, Quebec
(George) "Buddy" Guy, American blues guitarist (Stone Crazy), born in Lettsworth, Louisiana
Jimmy Cliff [Chambers], Jamaican reggae singer ("The Harder They Come"), born in St James, Colony of Jamaica
Finneas [O'Connell], American Grammy Award-winning singer-songwriter, record producer, and audio engineer (Billie Eilish), born in Los Angeles, California
Nicky Hayden, American motorcycle racer (MotoGP Champion 2006), born in Owensboro, Kentucky (d. 2017)
House of Burgesses in Virginia forms, first elected American governing body
George Eastmanshows the first amateur color motion pictures to guests at his New York house, includingThomas Edison
First Penguin Books published, starting the paperback revolution
New Zealand claims Ross Dependency in Antarctica
Beatles' "Yesterday... and Today" album goes to #1 and stays at #1 for 5 weeks
Indian power grid failure leaves over 300 million without electricity
Heavy metal bandMetallicarelease their single "Enter Sandman"
Richie AshburnandMike Schmidtenter baseball's Hall of Fame
The last "old style" Volkswagen Beetle rolls off the assembly line in Mexico
Henry Moore, English artist and sculptor (known for Three Piece Sculpture: Vertebrae), born in Castleford (d. 1986)
Victor Wong [Yee Keung Victor Wong], American actor (Big Trouble in Little China), born in San Francisco, California (d. 2001)
Bill Cartwright, American basketball center (NBA C'ship 1991, 92, 93 Chicago Bulls; NBA All-Star 1980 NY Knicks), born in Lodi, California
Ron Block, American Grammy Awrd-winning bluegrass banjo player, guitarist, and singer-songwriter (Alison Krauss & Union Station), born in Gardena, California
Terry Crews, American NFL linebacker (Washington Redskins) and actor (Brooklyn Nine-Nine), born in Flint, Michigan
Joey King, American actress (The Kissing Booth, The Act), born in Los Angeles, California
